lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Lucid Blocks Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Start by learning how exploration, fusion, and progression connect. Lucid Blocks rewards experimentation, so early progress comes from gathering unusual materials, testing combinations, and unlocking your personal Qualia.

steps.title: Explore Before You Commit
steps.description: Walk outward, mine common blocks, and keep any strange drops you find. Early progress is less about fixed quests and more about discovering what materials can become.

steps.title: Unlock Your Personal Qualia Early
steps.description: Your personal Qualia is one of the most important early milestones. It gives you a reliable place to regroup and build around.

steps.title: Use Osmium for Fast Early Progress
steps.description: Osmium is one of the most useful early materials. A popular early route is combining Osmium with sticks to make a Frost Pick for fast mining.

steps.title: Record Good Fusions
steps.description: Keep track of successful combinations in your journal. Lucid Blocks has many possible fusion paths, so writing down useful results saves a lot of time.

quickTips: Apotheosis does not require a fixed recipe list.
Infinite world generation means moving higher or deeper can reveal completely different materials.
Movement tools and utility gear become a major quality-of-life boost once you start unlocking them.
Getting lost early is normal, but useful tools, storage, and movement options snowball fast once your first good fusions start working.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Lucid Blocks Apotheosis Crafting
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Apotheosis is the game's unrestricted fusion system. Instead of memorizing a traditional recipe book, you combine materials, watch the result, and build your own crafting knowledge over time.

cards.name: Freeform Fusion
cards.description: Apotheosis lets you fuse together different items without needing to follow a fixed recipe list.

cards.name: Multiple Useful Outcomes
cards.description: Different materials often push toward certain results. Community discoveries show that similar inputs can still lead to practical tools, storage, or combat upgrades.

cards.name: Journal-First Crafting
cards.description: The best way to learn Apotheosis is to track your successful combinations. Your journal quickly becomes your own crafting reference.

cards.name: Community-Tested Early Fusions
cards.description: Popular early examples include Osmium plus Stick for a Frost Pick, Stick plus Tungsten Appendage for Azrael, and Azrael plus Magnetite for the Tendril Rapier.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Lucid Blocks Tools and Weapons
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Lucid Blocks mixes official utility gear with community-discovered early weapons. The best setup is usually a blend of fast mining, safe traversal, and one dependable combat option.

items.name: Frost Pick
items.description: A fast and affordable early pick made from Osmium and Stick. Players use it to speed up early material gathering.

items.name: Azrael
items.description: A practical early weapon route crafted from Stick and Tungsten Appendage.

items.name: Tendril Rapier
items.description: A stronger follow-up made by combining Azrael with Magnetite. It is one of the most useful early-mid combat paths shared by players.

items.name: Hookshot
items.description: An official traversal item that greatly improves movement and vertical exploration.

items.name: Bee Glider
items.description: An official movement item that helps cover space more safely and efficiently.

items.name: Ball Wand
items.description: An official ranged utility item that expands your combat and puzzle options.

items.name: Scuba Diving Gear
items.description: An official utility item for reaching areas that would otherwise slow down exploration.

items.name: Explosives
items.description: Officially highlighted as part of the core toolset and useful for breaking through threats or obstacles.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Lucid Blocks Storage and Inventory
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Inventory pressure starts early in Lucid Blocks, so building storage should be one of your first goals. Several real player-tested solutions already stand out.

solutions.name: Cache Cube
solutions.description: Cache Cubes are one of the earliest practical storage answers and are treated by players as the game's main chest-style container.

solutions.name: Cabinet
solutions.description: Cabinets are another confirmed storage option. A shared early recipe uses 1 Cut Wood and 1 String.

solutions.name: Personal Qualia
solutions.description: Once unlocked, your personal Qualia becomes a natural place to organize materials, craft in peace, and expand your base setup.

solutions.name: Journal Tracking
solutions.description: Recording useful combinations in your journal reduces wasted materials and makes inventory management much easier.

managementTips: Prioritize one dependable storage option before hoarding rare drops.
Build around your personal Qualia once it becomes available.
Keep crafting materials, combat tools, and movement gear separated so upgrades are easier to plan.
Use your journal as part of inventory management, not just crafting discovery.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Lucid Blocks Qualia and Base Building
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Qualia is one of the most important progression milestones in Lucid Blocks. It gives you a personal space to regroup, store materials, and gradually turn exploration progress into a real home base.

cards.name: Personal Qualia
cards.description: Unlocking a Qualia is an early milestone that gives you a more reliable place to return to while exploring the wider world.

cards.name: Storage-First Building
cards.description: Many players start using their Qualia as a storage hub before turning it into a larger base with organized crafting and building space.

cards.name: Creative Home Building
cards.description: Lucid Blocks supports creative building, and players are already using Qualia spaces to make houses and personalized layouts.

cards.name: Progression Hub
cards.description: A strong base helps you separate exploration loot, crafting materials, seeds, tools, and mobility gear so your next upgrades are easier to plan.

highlights: Use base space to support crafting and farming

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Lucid Blocks World Regions
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Lucid Blocks uses infinite world generation, so players keep discovering new environments and structures. A few named places are already showing up in achievements and community discussions.

regions.name: Fog Meadow
regions.description: Fog Meadow is already known among players as a tense area associated with hidden threats and dangerous encounters.

regions.name: Tiamana Leyline
regions.description: The tiamana leyline is important enough to have its own achievement milestone, making it one of the clearest named world discoveries so far.

regions.name: Clonaqualia
regions.description: Players are already using Clonaqualia as a place to build houses, store materials, and experiment with more permanent layouts.

regions.name: Essence Morass
regions.description: Essence Morass is being shared by players as a useful farming location, especially for fighting slimes and gaining experience.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Lucid Blocks Creatures and Enemies
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Lucid Blocks mixes surreal creatures with real danger. Official descriptions and community posts point to a world full of strange life, hostile threats, and a few standout encounters players remember quickly.

creatures.name: Manikins
creatures.description: Manikins are one of the officially named foes in the world and part of the game's surreal threat roster.

creatures.name: Squishy Gels
creatures.description: Squishy gels are another officially named enemy type and help define the game's strange biological style.

creatures.name: Higher-Dimensional Beings
creatures.description: These beings are presented as part of the world's more abstract and unsettling enemy lineup.

creatures.name: Colossal Spiders
creatures.description: Spiders are one of the most immediately feared enemies in community discussions and are clearly a memorable danger.

creatures.name: Bubblebear
creatures.description: Bubblebear is notable enough to have its own achievement, which makes it one of the clearest named creature encounters in the game.

creatures.name: Cute Vermin
creatures.description: Vermin are not only part of fusion progression, they can also be renamed, giving them a more personal role than standard enemies.

creatures.name: Blasphemous Creature
creatures.description: The blasphemous creature is tied to a dedicated combat achievement, marking it as a significant enemy encounter.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Lucid Blocks Mobility Gear
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Movement upgrades are one of the biggest quality-of-life jumps in Lucid Blocks. Once you unlock your first traversal tools, exploration becomes faster, safer, and much more rewarding.

items.name: Hookshot
items.description: The hookshot is one of the clearest movement milestones in the game and has its own dedicated achievement.

items.name: Glider Glove
items.description: The glider glove opens up much smoother travel and is another major progression milestone with its own achievement.

items.name: Bee Glider
items.description: Bee gliders are officially highlighted as part of the game's movement toolkit for covering space more efficiently.

items.name: Ball Wand
items.description: Ball wands are one of the officially highlighted special tools and expand how you move through or interact with the world.

items.name: Scuba Diving Gear
items.description: Scuba diving gear is officially listed as part of the core utility set for reaching areas that would otherwise be harder to explore.

items.name: Explosives
items.description: Explosives are both officially highlighted and linked to an achievement milestone, making them part of the game's core utility progression.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Lucid Blocks Farming and Growth
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Growth is part of real progression in Lucid Blocks. Farming is not only about planting and harvesting, but also about setting up repeatable material routes that keep your tools, weapons, and crafting options flowing.

sections.name: Seed Progression
sections.description: Planting and fertilizing seeds is a real milestone in the game, and harvesting a fully-grown nut is part of the same growth path.

sections.name: Material Farming
sections.description: A practical early farming loop is creating Mercury Capsules, which spawn small slimes that drop Tendrils and goo for repeatable crafting materials.

sections.name: Tool Sustain
sections.description: Resource farming becomes much stronger once you can consistently gather Osmium, glass shards, plants, and slime materials for follow-up crafts.

sections.name: Base Routine
sections.description: The best farming setup supports more than food. It should feed your storage, movement tools, weapon upgrades, and future fusion experiments.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Lucid Blocks Best Early Unlocks
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The strongest early unlocks are the ones that improve safety, storage, and movement. In Lucid Blocks, a few practical upgrades can make the entire rest of the game much smoother.

priorities.name: Personal Qualia
priorities.description: As soon as you get access to your personal Qualia, your run becomes much more stable. It acts as a reliable hub for storage, building, and regrouping.

priorities.name: Frost Pick
priorities.description: A fast and cheap early mining tool made from Osmium and Stick. It is one of the most useful upgrades for accelerating early resource collection.

priorities.name: Cache Cube
priorities.description: Storage pressure starts early, so getting your first dependable chest-style container is a major quality-of-life upgrade.

priorities.name: Hookshot
priorities.description: The hookshot is one of the clearest movement milestones in the game and quickly makes exploration faster and safer.

priorities.name: Glider Glove
priorities.description: The glider glove adds another major mobility boost and makes long-distance movement far more efficient.

priorities.name: Projectile Wand or Bombs
priorities.description: A ranged tool or explosive option gives you a safer answer to dangerous enemies and trial-style encounters.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Lucid Blocks Achievement Tracker
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Lucid Blocks currently has 19 Steam achievements, and they naturally split into exploration, crafting, mobility, combat, and growth progression.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Lucid Blocks Singleplayer and Platform FAQ
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: These are the core platform and access questions players keep asking before buying Lucid Blocks.

faqs.question: Is Lucid Blocks singleplayer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lucid Blocks is a singleplayer game.

faqs.question: Is multiplayer or co-op planned?
faqs.answer: No. Multiplayer is not planned for release or future updates.

faqs.question: Where can I play Lucid Blocks?
faqs.answer: It is currently available on Steam for Windows.

faqs.question: How much does Lucid Blocks cost?
Does Lucid Blocks support controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the menus are not designed around it.

faqs.question: Does Lucid Blocks work on Steam Deck?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 or 9.0.4 is the commonly recommended setup for Steam Deck.

faqs.question: Does Lucid Blocks work on Linux?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 is reported to work well for Linux and Steam Deck.

faqs.question: Will Mac or Android be supported?
faqs.answer: They may be supported in the future, but they are not current launch platforms.

faqs.question: Does Lucid Blocks have translations?
faqs.answer: No. There are currently no translations planned.

faqs.question: Will Lucid Blocks keep getting updates?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Lucid Blocks Steam Deck and Controller
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Lucid Blocks can run on Steam Deck, but the default setup may need adjustment. Keyboard and mouse remains the smoother option for menus and general play.

faqs.question: Does Lucid Blocks work on Steam Deck?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 works well, and Proton 9.0.4 is a commonly shared fix for Steam Deck issues.

faqs.question: What should I do if Steam Deck controls are not working?
faqs.answer: Force compatibility and switch the game to Proton 9.0.4. The default configuration may not accept controller input correctly.

faqs.question: Does Lucid Blocks support controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the game's menus are not designed around it.

faqs.question: Is Linux support possible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 is reported to work well for Linux and Steam Deck.

faqs.question: What is the best input setup?
faqs.answer: Keyboard and mouse is the smoothest option for menu navigation and general moment-to-moment play.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Lucid Blocks Settings and Accessibility
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Lucid Blocks already has a few practical settings and compatibility notes that matter immediately when players first launch the game.

settings.name: Display Settings
settings.description: Display Settings includes an option that lets players hide the on-screen hands.

settings.name: Graphics Compatibility
settings.description: Vulkan support is required for the graphics to render properly. Systems without Vulkan can run into crashes and severe graphical glitches.

settings.name: Input Preference
settings.description: Controller support exists, but keyboard and mouse gives a smoother experience because menus are not built around controller use.

settings.name: Language Support
settings.description: The current setup is English-only, and no translations are planned right now.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Lucid Blocks Updates and Patch Notes
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Lucid Blocks is already receiving quick post-launch fixes and small usability updates.

entries.title: Update 2.8.2
entries.description: Renamed "awaken" to "exit" in the main menu.

entries.title: Small Patch for Start-Up Crashes
entries.description: A dedicated patch was released for players whose game was crashing on launch.

entries.title: Post-Launch Content Support
entries.description: Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Lucid Blocks Crash Fix and Troubleshooting
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Most immediate launch and crash issues should be checked in a simple order: patch level, graphics support, platform compatibility, and logs.

steps.title: Install the Latest Crash Hotfix
steps.description: If the game is crashing on start, update first and test again with the latest start-up crash patch.

steps.title: Check Vulkan Support
steps.description: Lucid Blocks requires Vulkan support. Systems without Vulkan can suffer from crashes or major rendering problems.

steps.title: Use Proton 9.0.4 on Steam Deck
steps.description: If Steam Deck input or launch behavior is broken, force compatibility and switch to Proton 9.0.4.

steps.title: Collect Crash Logs
steps.description: Open Windows Run, paste %APPDATA%, go to lucid blocks/logs, and gather the .txt files for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Report the Issue Properly
steps.description: Share the logs and device details through the official Discord bug-reports forum or on the Steam community page.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Lucid Blocks

questions.question: Is Lucid Blocks a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Lucid Blocks is a singleplayer-only game with no multiplayer or co-op planned.

questions.question: What is the Apotheosis system in Lucid Blocks?
questions.answer: Apotheosis is Lucid Blocks' freeform fusion crafting system. Instead of following fixed recipes, you combine materials and discover your own crafting paths.

questions.question: Where can I play Lucid Blocks?
questions.answer: Lucid Blocks is currently available on Steam for Windows. Linux and Steam Deck support is available through Proton 9.0.

questions.question: How much does Lucid Blocks cost?
Does Lucid Blocks support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the menus are not designed around it. Keyboard and mouse is the smoother option.

questions.question: Will Lucid Blocks receive updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.

whySteps.step2Description: Match a human type to your home environment and care capacity
whySteps.step3Description: Use type rankings to weigh strengths, needs, and long-term fit

whySteps.step2Description: Lay out rooms that match your human's type, traits, and routines
whySteps.step3Description: Personalize the home to boost comfort and long-term happiness
